section.heading: Commission to serve against friendly nation

content.p: Any citizen of the United States who, within the jurisdiction thereof, accepts and exercises a commission to serve a foreign prince, state, colony, district, or people, in war, against any prince, state, colony, district, or people, with whom the United States is at peace, shall be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than three years, or both.

note.heading: Historical and Revision Notes
note.p: Mandatory punishment provision was rephrased in the alternative.
Minor changes in phraseology were made.

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 1994—Pub. L. 103–322 substituted “fined under this title” for “fined not more than $2,000”.
